content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
April 22, 1976

Senator David A. Roberti
State Capitol
Sacramento CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 76-053

Dear Senator Roberti:

Thank you for your letter of April 6, 1976, requesting an opinion concerning whether, consistent with Government Code Section 89001, you can forward the results of a questionnaire and a "short explanatory form letter" to persons who requested that they be sent the results of the questionnaire. Because your request does not raise a substantial question of interpretation under the Political Reform Act, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful.

The definition of "mass mailing" states that a mass mailing "does not include a form letter or other act which is sent in response to a request, letter or other inquiry." Government Code Section 82041.5. Accordingly, in responding to a request for the results of the questionnaire, you are not sending a mass mailing within the meaning of Government Code Section 89001. Moreover, a short form letter which contains nothing more than an explanation of the results also is permissible. I would caution you, however, that the explanatory letter cannot include commentary about your views relative to the results or other statements which would be designed to further your candidacy.

If you wish to appeal the denial of your request, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. If you have any further questions, please contact Kenneth Finney, Chief of the Commission's Legal Division.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DHL:pvp

STATE SENATOR
DAVID A. ROBERTI
MAJORITY FLOOR LEADER

California Legislature
LOS ANGELES COUNTY

April 6, 1976


Mr. Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

REQUEST FOR OPINION

In January of 1976, a questionnaire was sent to the residents of the
23rd Senatorial District where I am now a candidate for reelection
to the State Senate. On the questionnaire, a box was provided asking
the respondents to check it if they would like to have the results of
the questionnaire sent to them.

My District Office informed me that questionnaires will soon be tallied.
I would like to send the results to those who so desire them. There
are 5,896 involved.

I would like to know whether I can do this with a short explanatory
form letter and a form tally sheet and not be in violation of the
Fair Political Practices Act.

Enclosed is a copy of the questionnaire.

Sincerely,

DAVID A. ROBERTI

DAR:mbg

Encl

Your response to this questionnaire will assist me in better serve your views. Please fill in all the enclosed questions and form, place in the return envelope, seal it and mail to my district office.

Senator Robert
Requests Your Opinion

HIS        HERS
Yes  No    Yes  No
□    □     □    □

1) Would you favor raising the gas tax two cents to fund mass transit 
   systems in California?
                                                          Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

2) Do you believe that nuclear power is safe?            Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

3) Should the development of nuclear power be hastened to meet our
   energy needs?                                          Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

4) Would you favor a solution to the medical malpractice crisis that puts
   limits on the amount of money you could recover in court for pain and
   suffering due to malpractice?                          Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

5) Would you favor the government acting as an insurer to doctors to
   deal with the malpractice situation even if this means your taxes
   would be increased a small amount?                     Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

6) Would you support state controls on insurance company rates as a
   solution to the malpractice problem?                   Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

7) If it takes a simple majority vote of the Legislature to raise taxes on
   Californians but a two-thirds vote to increase them on corporations,
   would you favor reducing the two-thirds requirement on corporations
   to a simple majority?                                  Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

8) Would you support raising the tax on a six pack of beer or a bottle of
   wine two cents to fund programs treating alcoholism?   Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

9) Would you support a program of financial assistance to elderly
   renters on fixed incomes even if it meant a small tax increase for
   others?                                                Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □
                                                          Yes  No    Yes  No
10) Do you feel inflation is a greater problem than unemployment?
                                                          □    □     □    □
                                                          Yes  No    Yes  No
    Should the State provide public jobs to deal with unemployment?
                                                          □    □     □    □

12) If the death penalty were used to punish those who commit serious
    crimes, do you believe that the number of crimes committed would
    decrease?                                             Yes  No    Yes  No
                                                          □    □     □    □

13) Should high school girls be allowed to compete in minimal contact sports with boys such as baseball, soccer or basketball?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

14) Do you believe that high school girls should be able to participate in full contact sports with boys such as football, water polo, and wrestling?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

15) Do you favor busing school children for purposes of integration away from their neighborhoods without their parents' consent?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

16) If there is a possibility that aerosol sprays harm our atmosphere with the possibility of increased risk of skin cancer and noted experts disagree on the theory, would you favor banning the use of these aerosols in California?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

17) California's Primary Election is currently in June and the General Election is in November. Would you favor shortening the election time by moving the Primary back to September?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

18) Would you want election day on a Saturday rather than Tuesday?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

19) Should the government provide health insurance for every citizen for major illnesses?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

20) Would you favor elimination of all property taxes by increasing income taxes by the proportional amount?

Yes No
☐ ☐

Yes No
☐ ☐

If you want the results of this questionnaire sent to you, please check this box. ☐

Some of the best ideas for new laws come directly from my constituents. If you believe that we need a new law or should change an existing one, please explain below.

_________________________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________________________

Please answer the following questions only if you choose to.

Name ___________________________________________________________________________

Street _________________________________________________ Zip Code ________________

Age _____________ Occupation _____________________________________________________
